# Database connection example

```{python}
from database import (
    XlDatabase,
    Equipment,
    AnalysisType,
    AnalysisResult,
    expression
)
```

Connecting to the database:

```{python}
db = XlDatabase()
```

Adding new elements with `Insert` method:

```{python}
sem = Equipment(
    name         = "SEM",
    model        = "Model XYZ",
    manufacturer = "SEM Corp",
    serialNumber = "12345"
)

result = AnalysisResult(
    type      = AnalysisType.SEM,
    machineId = sem.Id,
    sampleId  = "Sample123",
    data      = "Analysis data goes here..."
)

print(f"new equipment = {db.InsertEquipment(sem)}")
print(f"new equipment = {db.InsertEquipment(sem)}")

print(f"new analysis result = {db.InsertAnalysisResult(result)}")
print(f"new analysis result = {db.InsertAnalysisResult(result)}")
```

Print an element:

```{python}
print(sem)
```

```{python}
print(result)
```

List all elements from a collection:

```{python}
list(db.GetAllEquipment())
```

```{python}
list(db.GetAllAnalysisResults())
```

More advanced query with filtering, sorting and limiting results:

```{python}
items = list(
    db.EquipmentCollection.Query()
        .Where(expression("$.Name = 'SEM'"))
        .OrderBy(expression("$.Name"))
        .Limit(5)
        .ToList()
)
print(items[0])
```

Check if internal references are working:

```{python}
items = list(
    db.AnalysisResultsCollection.Query()
        .Where(expression("$.Type = 'SEM'"))
        .OrderBy(expression("$.CreatedOn", ascending=False))
        .Limit(5)
        .ToList()
)

items = list(
    db.EquipmentCollection.Query()
        .Where(expression("$.Id = @objId", objId=items[0].MachineId))
        .ToList()
)
print(items[0])
```
